scope: Responses API auto-tool streaming empty XML tool-call fail-closed behavior
root_cause: Current parser/filtering already drops the empty exec_command call because required argument cmd is missing, so it does not emit arguments={}. The remaining source bug was in Responses streaming auto-tool finalization: after a buffered invalid tool call was dropped, the no-tool path used accumulated_content as final visible text, which leaked the raw <tool_call>/<function=...> markup into response.output_text.done and response.completed.output_text.
fix: When no tool call survives parsing but the full stream contains tool-call markers, the Responses no-tool finalization path now strips native tool markup from final visible text. It does not synthesize arguments, does not convert malformed XML into a tool call, and does not disable reasoning.

proof.parser_warning: Dropping parsed tool call for exec_command because required argument cmd is missing or empty

verification_commands: .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_server.py -k 'streaming_responses_auto_empty_xml_tool_call_strips_final_markup or streaming_responses_preamble_empty_xml_tool_call_never_emits_empty_arguments or streaming_responses_required_empty_xml_tool_call_is_rejected or tool_parser_drops_empty_xml_call_and_strips_markup_for_nonstream_paths or streaming_responses_tool_call_arguments_survive_buffering or streaming_responses_tool_call_uses_next_output_index_without_text'
boundaries: This is a source/API fail-closed and markup-cleanup fix.
It does not claim same-model direct/gateway/tunnel live raw SSE is complete.
It does not sign, notarize, package, publish, or release anything.
It does not touch N2 JANG_1L.
